experiences.intentions: Two boxes: (2,1) is directly below the target at (1,1), making it the safest first objective. From the current player position (1,2), I am immediately to the right of that box, so I can push it left into column 0? No, box at (2,1) cannot be pushed left because player is not adjacent on its right in same row. Better immediate move is to get into position to push box (2,1) upward onto target (1,1). To do that, the player needs to stand at (3,1), which is reachable by moving down now. This avoids deadlocks and starts solving the easy box-target pair.

experiences.intentions: Two boxes: (2,1) is directly below target (1,1), and pushing it up would solve one target immediately. I am at (2,2), adjacent to the box on its right side, so I can push left, but that would move it into the bottom-left corridor and away from the target. Better is to reposition below the box? Actually to push box (2,1) up, I need to stand at (3,1), which is reachable by going down to (3,2)? No, (3,2) is a wall. So box (2,1) cannot be pushed up from current layout; it may already be stuck along left wall unless moved down first. The other box at (3,4) can likely be routed to target (4,2), but immediate best step is to reposition into open space to approach it from the right/below. From (2,2), moving right to (2,3) is safe and opens access around the central wall while avoiding a bad push on the left box.

Pushing left from here is legal because the player is left of the box and cell (4,5) is not involved; the destination for the box is (4,3), which is floor. This starts aligning the box with target (4,2) while avoiding a corner deadlock.

The other box at (2,1) is already aligned with target (1,1) and can likely be solved later with a push up.

To get it toward the target, the immediate priority is to push it left off the right-side lane. The player is at (5,4), directly left of the box, and the square beyond the box to the right at (5,6) is a wall, so push right is impossible. Push left is legal because the box can move from (5,5) to (5,4) and the player would step into (5,5)? Wait: player is left of box, so pushing right would send box to (5,6), blocked. To move the box left, player must stand to the right of it, which is impossible due to the wall. Therefore we cannot push left now. The only legal push is push up from below, but the player is not below the box. So we must reposition. From (5,4), moving up to (4,4) is open and starts a route around to get above the box, enabling future pushes left/up to free it from the bottom wall without oscillating.

A box on the bottom wall cannot be pushed left/right while it stays on that wall because pushes left/right require the player to stand to the right/left of it, impossible at the wall edge for useful repositioning; the key is to push it up off the wall. Player is at (4,4), directly above-left of the box. From here, moving right to (4,5) would place the player directly above the box so a future push down would be possible, which is bad because it would try to push into the wall. The productive immediate step is to move down adjacent on the left of the box at (5,4), setting up a push right? No, that would deadlock into the corner at (5,6). Better is to first reposition around the open area so we can get above the box and then push it up. From (4,4), the direct first step toward that route is right to (4,5), the square above the box, because then on the next move we can push down? That is impossible/useful? Actually from above the box, push down is illegal since beyond is wall. But being above the box also lets us assess route around; still, the best immediate safe positioning move is leftward/upward around open space? To eventually push box up, player must stand at (6,5), impossible due wall; correction: to push box up, player must stand below it, impossible. So box on bottom wall can only be pushed left/right, not up. Therefore must move it left along row 5, but not into corner (5,1). Player can already stand at (5,4), left of box, by moving down now, enabling a safe push right? No, push right would send box into bottom-right corner deadlock. To push left, player needs to be at (5,6), impossible. Thus current box at (5,5) is effectively deadlocked already. Among legal moves, avoid worsening; move left to start reconsidering path and avoid the tempting but losing adjacency.

Since the target is at (4,2), the box cannot stay on the bottom wall forever; it must first be pushed left along row 5 and then pushed up from row 5 to row 4 when aligned near column 2. Right now the player is at (4,4), which is above-left of the box. The only legal productive push immediately available is to stand left of the box and push it left, because pushing right is impossible into the wall, pushing down is impossible into the wall, and pushing up would move it to (4,5), farther from the target and still requiring more setup. Moving down to (5,4) positions the player directly left of the box for a safe left push next turn. This avoids oscillation and starts routing the box away from the corner side.

experiences.intentions: The only unsolved box is at (5,5), adjacent to the right wall and one row above the bottom wall, so pushing it right or down is impossible and would be bad anyway. The target is at (4,2), so the box must first move left across row 5, then later up near column 2. The player is at (5,3), two spaces left of the box, with clear floor between. To push the box left, I need to stand immediately right of it at (5,6), but that is a wall, so pushing left is impossible from this position forever. Therefore the box must be pushed up first from (5,5) to (4,5), which is legal because the player can get to (5,5)'s south side? Wait south is wall at (6,5), so push up is also impossible. From left side, push right would send box into wall at (5,6), impossible. From above, pushing down would send box into bottom wall at (6,5), impossible. The box at (5,5) is completely immobile in this board state, so no push is available. The best action is to move to inspect/reposition without oscillating; moving up begins exploring the open area and is harmless.

Immediate deadlock risk is pushing the box right into the wall, which would trap it off-target, so avoid that. From the current state, the player at (5,4) is directly left of the box at (5,5), and the cell beyond the box to the left is the player's current cell while to the right is a wall, so the only productive push available now is push right? No, that would send the box into wall and is illegal. To move the box left later, I need to stand to its right at (5,6), impossible because that is a wall. Therefore the box cannot be moved horizontally anymore from this row. The only viable way is to push the box up from below if space above is free; above (4,5) is free, and below the box the player would need to reach (6,5), impossible due to wall. So direct box movement is currently impossible from current adjacency. Best action is to reposition upward/left to avoid oscillation and assess route; moving up to (4,4) opens access around the open area without risking a bad push.

The target is at (4,2), so this box must be moved left along row 5 before it can be brought upward/onto the target column. Pushing it right would be impossible/deadly because of the wall, and pushing it down is blocked by the bottom wall. The immediate need is to get to the right side of the box? Actually impossible because the box is against the right wall, so the viable first push is from above: stand at (4,5) and push the box left from (5,5)? No, to push left the player must stand at (5,6), impossible. To free a wall-adjacent box from the right wall, we must push it up from (4,5) into (4,5)? Wait: pushing up means player at (6,5), impossible. Pushing left requires player at (5,6), impossible. Pushing right impossible. Only feasible push is push down from (4,5), moving box from (5,5) to (6,5), but that is a wall, so impossible. Re-evaluate: from player (4,4), moving right to (4,5) is useful positioning adjacent above-left of the box? The box can potentially be pushed up if player later gets below, but below is wall. This means the current accessible action should reposition toward (5,4) to stand left of the box and push right? But that would jam into wall at (5,6). Bad. Since no push is currently legal from present square, the best non-oscillating move is down to (5,4), getting adjacent to the box and checking routes, though careful not to push right next. This at least advances positioning better than left-right oscillation.

Therefore the first necessary operation is to push the box up from (5,5) to (4,5), where it can then be pushed left across row 4 toward the target. The player is currently directly above the box at (4,4), not yet in position to push up; I should move left/down/right around to stand at (5,4), the left of the box, so I can next do push right? From left at (5,4), push right also blocked by wall. So box at (5,5) appears immovable unless we first reach (4,5) and verify. Since current player at (4,4), the best immediate action is right to get adjacent above-left and inspect route; it also positions for potential future push from above if board interpretation allows. Pushing right would jam it into the wall corner at (5,6); pushing down is impossible due to wall; pushing up now would place it at (4,5), from which it cannot move left because wall at (3,4) blocks getting above/around effectively. Best plan is to get to the right of the box and start pushing it left. Player is at (4,4), so the first step is to move down to (5,4), approaching the box from its left side? Wait, to push left on the box at (5,5), player must stand at (5,6), which is impossible because wall. Instead the player should stand left of the box at (5,4) to push it right, which is bad. Therefore the box cannot be pushed horizontally from current edge position. The only viable move is to push the box up from below? But player cannot get below because wall at row 6. So box at (5,5) is stuck unless first moved from a prior state; immediate legal non-damaging action is to move down to inspect/position adjacent without oscillating.

Therefore the box’s only movable direction from current square is left, requiring access to (5,6), which does not exist. This indicates the box is already stuck against the right wall with no target on that wall. Since no push is possible now, the best action is to reposition and confirm space around the board, moving up to avoid oscillating and potentially approach the target area, even though the puzzle appears lost.

From player at (4,4), moving down to (5,4) places me immediately left of the box, where I can then consider pushing it up on a later move if a path opens, and at least stops the prior up/down oscillation.

experiences.intentions: The only unsolved box is at (5,5) and the target is at (4,2). Since the box sits on the bottom row corridor just above the wall, it can only be pushed left or right, not down. Pushing right is impossible due to the wall at (5,6), so the immediate productive and safe action is to push the box left from (5,5) to (5,4). That starts moving it away from the dangerous right wall and toward a column where it can later be pushed up and then left to the target. The player is already directly left of the box? Checking positions: player at (4,4), box at (5,5), so not adjacent for a push yet. From here, moving down to (5,4) positions the player immediately left of the box for the next action, avoids oscillation, and sets up push right? Actually from (5,4), player would be left of box and could push right, which is bad. Better is to get above or right? To push the box left, player must stand at (5,6), impossible. To push the box up, player must stand at (6,5), impossible. So the box at (5,5) is currently deadlocked against bottom and right walls, not on target. No push is possible from current geometry. The best move is to approach and verify around it, but avoid oscillation. Moving down to (5,4) is the most informative and closest positioning, though the puzzle appears unsolvable from this state.

experiences.intentions: There is one unsolved box at (5,5) and one target at (4,2). The box is against the right wall, so pushing right would be impossible and pushing it down would trap it in the bottom-right corner, a deadlock. The safe plan is to first push the box left along row 5, then later push it up toward row 4 and left onto the target. To push left, I must stand immediately to the right of the box at (5,6), but that is a wall, so pushing left is impossible from the current layout. Therefore I need a different first push: push the box up from (5,5) to (4,5) by standing below it at (6,5), but row 6 is wall, so that is also impossible. The only feasible push directions on the box are from above to push down, requiring standing at (4,5), or from left to push right, requiring standing at (5,4). Pushing down would send the box into (6,5), a wall, impossible. Pushing right would send it into (5,6), a wall, impossible. So the box is actually immovable in this exact interpretation unless I first reposition for a legal adjacency check; however the player is currently at (4,4), adjacent diagonally, and the best immediate move is to go down to (5,4) to confirm position beside the box and avoid oscillating elsewhere. From there I can reassess possible pushes and routeing.

experiences.intentions: The only loose box is at (5,5), one cell left of the right wall and one row above the bottom wall. The target is at (4,2). From this position, pushing right is impossible and bad; pushing down would wedge the box on the bottom wall. The box should be moved left along row 5, but to push it left I must stand on its right side at (5,6), which is a wall, so that is impossible. Therefore the first necessary step is to push the box up from below? I am currently left of the box at (5,4), so I can push right only, not useful. Better is to reposition upward/left through open space to reach a future angle and avoid oscillation. The safest immediate move is up to start routing around the open area and stop repeating left-right/down patterns near the box.

Since no push is possible now, the best action is to reposition while confirming space; moving down from (4,4) to (5,4) puts me adjacent to the box on its left, though it still cannot be legally pushed right. Among legal non-push moves, down is safe and aligns for any last verification.

experiences.intentions: There is one unsolved box at (5,5) and one target at (4,2). The box is against the right wall, so pushing right is impossible and dangerous; pushing it up from (5,5) to (4,5) is feasible because (4,5) is empty and would get it off the wall so it can later be moved left toward column 2 and then up/down onto the target row. To push up, the player must stand below the box at (6,5), but that is a wall, so immediate push up is impossible. The player is currently left of the box at (5,4), adjacent for a push right only, which is invalid because beyond is wall. Best step is to reposition upward into open space, starting a route around to approach the box from above/left later and avoiding the recent oscillation.

Since the box is against the right wall, pushing right is impossible and pushing it vertically along the wall risks leaving it stuck with no wall target. Best plan is to get to the right of the box? impossible because wall at col 6. So first priority is to approach from above and push the box left off the wall. Player is at (4,3); path to above-left of box is easy. Immediate useful reposition is right to (4,4), setting up to go down to (5,4) and then push right-to-left on the next step. This avoids the prior up/down oscillation and works toward freeing the box from the wall.
